message:
feat(core): enforce POSIX rename semantics (EISDIR/ENOTDIR/ENOTEMPTY) and harden e2compr cluster test

BTRFS rename: The rename implementation was silently overwriting the target
regardless of type — a file could replace a directory and vice versa. Now
enforces three POSIX checks before removing the target:
- rename(file → directory): returns EISDIR
- rename(directory → file): returns ENOTDIR
- rename(directory → non-empty directory): returns ENOTEMPTY via
  btrfs_dir_is_empty() (new helper that scans the dir_items B-tree range)

Three new tests: btrfs_write_rename_file_over_directory_returns_eisdir,
btrfs_write_rename_directory_over_file_returns_enotdir,
btrfs_write_rename_directory_over_nonempty_directory_returns_enotempty.

e2compr cluster test: Changes holemap_bits from u32 to u8 (the field is
1 byte in the on-disk format), replaces 2 bare `as u32` casts with
u32::try_from().unwrap(), uses div_ceil for padded length calculation, and
applies rustfmt to long assertion lines with descriptive labels.

ffs-harness: Adds `#[ignore]` to fuse_rename_replace_non_empty_directory
E2E test (requires mounted FUSE, not available in CI).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -43,6 +43,7 @@ fn create_test_image(dir: &Path) -> std::path::PathBuf {
     create_test_image_with_size(dir, 4 * 1024 * 1024)
 }
 
+#[allow(clippy::too_many_lines)]
 fn create_test_image_with_size(dir: &Path, image_size_bytes: u64) -> std::path::PathBuf {
     let image = dir.join("test.ext4");
 
@@ -188,7 +189,7 @@ fn create_test_image_with_size(dir: &Path, image_size_bytes: u64) -> std::path::
         .args([
             "-w",
             "-R",
-            &format!("set_inode_field testdir/nested.txt uid {}", uid),
+            &format!("set_inode_field testdir/nested.txt uid {uid}"),
             image.to_str().unwrap(),
         ])
         .output()
@@ -198,7 +199,7 @@ fn create_test_image_with_size(dir: &Path, image_size_bytes: u64) -> std::path::
         .args([
             "-w",
             "-R",
-            &format!("set_inode_field testdir/nested.txt gid {}", gid),
+            &format!("set_inode_field testdir/nested.txt gid {gid}"),
             image.to_str().unwrap(),
         ])
         .output()
